title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple explores a new residential development, walking through Aubrey Street, Dalton Street, and Walcot Street. Observing the colorful houses and the diverse residents, she notes that despite the modern setting, human nature and social patterns remain unchanged. While on Carisbrook Street, she observes a young couple, Lily and Harry, discussing a house. Lily leans out of a second-story window to view the scenery, causing a loose wooden board to slide out from under her. Harry fails to assist her as she struggles to cling to the wall. Witnessing this, Miss Marple approaches Lily and warns her not to marry Harry, advising her to find someone more reliable in times of danger.

As Miss Marple attempts to leave, she trips over loose stones and falls. A plump woman named Heather Baddock rushes out to assist her and invites her into her home for tea. Inside, Heather, who claims to have served on a first aid team during the war, insists on adding four sugar cubes to Miss Marple's tea to treat the shock of the fall. Heather's husband, Arthur Baddock, joins them. During the conversation, Heather recognizes Miss Marple, identifying her as the woman known for investigating murder cases. Heather mentions rumors from the local Bingo Club regarding a murder in the village and references a previous murder at Gossington Hall, expressing her belief that such places would be haunted.
